article_title: Chiesi to Buy KalVista for $1.9 Billion in Its Biggest-Ever Deal, Wrapping the First Oral HAE Pill Into a Growing Rare Disease Portfolio

content_preview.title: Chiesi to Buy KalVista for $1.9 Billion in Its Biggest-Ever Deal, Wrapping the First Oral HAE Pill Into a Growing Rare Disease Portfolio
content_preview.summary: Italian drugmaker Chiesi will pay $27 a share for KalVista Pharmaceuticals to acquire Ekterly, the first oral on-demand treatment for hereditary angioedema, in a deal expected to close in the third quarter.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Chiesi Farmaceutici, the Italian privately held drugmaker, said it will acquire KalVista Pharmaceuticals for about $1.9 billion, the largest acquisition in the company's nearly 100-year history. The centerpiece of the deal is Ekterly (sebetralstat), the first oral, on-demand therapy approved for hereditary angioedema, a rare genetic swelling disorder long managed with injectables.

Under terms announced on April 29, Chiesi will pay $27 per share in cash for KalVista, [according to B...

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid biotech M&A News article (704 words). Clean structure, appropriate balance of headline facts, market context, and unknowns. Good cross-reference to prior Intellia coverage.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core deal facts verified across BioPharma Dive and STAT: $1.9B, $27/share, ~40% premium to Tuesday close, Ekterly as oral on-demand HAE pill, July approval, 20% U.S. patient share through February, Stifel/RBC analyst sourcing, CSL/Ionis competing injections, Pharvaris and Intellia context. Two of the bot's four sources (Bloomberg, FiercePharma) are bot-blocked, so the FiercePharma-specific details (36%/April-28 benchmark, July 2025 FDA date, age 12+, $49M sales, €6B target, 'biggest in 100-year history') and the Bloomberg 30-day-VWAP claim cannot be directly confirmed but are individually plausible and consistent with the verified sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. Honest framing of competitive landscape. Cross-reference to prior Intellia coverage (April 25 article) is well-placed.
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. The earlier Intellia HAE article gives this acquisition story narrative continuity.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Headline deal facts ($1.9B, $27/share, ~40% premium, Ekterly as oral HAE pill, July approval, 20% market share, analyst commentary, competitive landscape) all verified verbatim against BioPharma Dive and STAT. The FiercePharma- and Bloomberg-only details are unverifiable due to 403 bot-blocking, but the substance of the article is well-supported elsewhere and the framing is consistent with other industry coverage. Cross-reference to prior Intellia article is a good archive-building move.
